channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Claims Progress in Talks and Extends Strait of Hormuz Deadline
item.description: President Trump has threatened to attack Iran’s power plants unless it fully reopens the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, by April 6. Israel on Friday launched more strikes on Iran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Signature to Appear on Dollar Bills
item.description: President Trump’s signature will be added to U.S. paper currency later this year, the Treasury Department said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The addition of President Trump’s signature to dollars is the latest example of his emblazoning national institutions with his personal brand.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 27, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Picture Prompt: Two Reflections
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Maria Mavropoulou for The New York Times

item.title: Can a Democrat Make Headway With Trump? New York’s Governor Is Trying.
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul of New York has demonstrated her ability to negotiate with the president and his team on thorny issues like immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Benjamin Cleeton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Even as Gov. Kathy Hochul tries to find common ground with President Trump, she has also tried to draw attention to ICE raids in New York that have resulted in mass arrests and separations of families.

item.title: Tracking Trump’s Efforts to Reshape Cultural Institutions
item.description: Jennifer Schuessler, a culture reporter who writes about intellectual life, is now covering President Trump’s attempts to amend the presentation of American history.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Wisniewski for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A group of teachers posting signs on a former exhibit about slavery at the Independence National Historical Park in Philadelphia.

item.title: Nepal’s New Prime Minister, Former Rapper Balendra Shah, Sworn In
item.description: A leaked report on the deadly violence and mass arson that broke out last year has put the new leader under pressure to ensure accountability.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Binod Ghimire

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Balendra Shah, right, with Rabi Lamichhane, the founder of his party, campaigning in Kathmandu, Nepal, last month.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘octave’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 31 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: octave
item.description: This word has appeared in 31 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: The Faroe Islands, Wary After Greenland, Vote for Change
item.description: Statehood had been a key issue in this tiny Danish archipelago before President Trump threatened Greenland. Now, Faroese voters are focused more on their own economy than geopolitics.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Regin Winther Poulsen

media:content.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Faroe Islands flag being raised in November in front of a governmental building in Torshavn, the capital.

item.title: Europa teme que Rusia prepare el envío de drones a Irán
item.description: Se trataría de tecnología derivada de drones iraníes usados por Rusia en Ucrania, ahora adaptados para un nuevo conflicto.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger and Ivan Nechepurenko
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Fiscales ucranianos de crímenes de guerra con un dron ruso del tipo Shahed, en la región ucraniana de Járkov el año pasado. Aunque Irán inicialmente suministró cientos de drones en 2022, Rusia construyó después su propia planta de producción y evolucionó el diseño.

item.title: ¿Una infancia sin teléfono? Un pueblo irlandés lo está haciendo realidad
item.description: En Greystones han aprendido que solo un esfuerzo conjunto para dejar de lado los dispositivos podría acabar con el argumento de los chicos de que “todo el mundo tiene uno”.
item.dc:creator: Sally McGrane and Therese Aherne

media:content.media:description: En Irlanda, el primogénito promedio adquiere un teléfono inteligente alrededor de los 9 años, mientras que sus hermanos pequeños suelen tenerlo antes, según un estudio reciente.

item.title: How to Straighten Out the Hormuz Bottleneck
item.description: Nations are looking for alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Medlock III
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by Kim Hoeckele for The New York Times; source photograph via Getty Images

item.title: The War in Ukraine Transformed Conflict. Iran Is the Proof.
item.description: Open-ended military conflict in Europe could, at some point, intersect with open-ended military conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kimmage
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Says He Will Order TSA Agents to Be Paid
item.description: President Trump said on Truth Social that he would order pay for T.S.A. agents, who have worked for weeks without compensation, as a stalemate in Congress over funding has brought chaos to airports.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

item.title: Same Shows, Different Prices: Broadway vs. the West End
item.description: Michael Paulson reports from New York, and Alex Marshall reports from London, to compare theater prices for the same shows on both sides of the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson, Alex Marshall, Gabriel Blanco, Laura Salaberry and Daniel Bateman

item.title: Trump vuelve a ampliar el plazo a Irán sobre el estrecho de Ormuz ante la caída de las bolsas
item.description: El presidente Trump amenazó por primera vez a Irán para que abriera el estrecho el sábado, y dijo que destruiría sus centrales eléctricas si no cumplía en 48 horas. Luego, emitió una prórroga hasta el viernes. Este es el segundo aplazamiento.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Adam Rasgon, Eshe Nelson and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump, con el secretario de Estado Marco Rubio, a la izquierda, y el secretario de Defensa Pete Hegseth, el jueves en la Casa Blanca. Trump dijo que si Irán no negociaba, “seguiremos haciéndolos estallar”.

item.title: In Sleepy Town on Strait of Hormuz, War Rages Just Over Horizon
item.description: For centuries, an Omani exclave has been defined by a peculiar duality: rugged isolation and proximity to one of the world’s most important trade routes.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Theater Tickets Are Cheaper in London Than New York. What Gives?
item.description: For half the price of a great seat at a Broadway show, you can see “Paddington” in the West End (if you can find a ticket) and snack on a marmalade sandwich.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson and Alex Marshall

item.title: The German Military Tightens Its Social Media Rules
item.description: Military members sharing clips on Instagram and TikTok have helped recruit badly needed new soldiers, but Bundeswehr officials said they are concerned about security.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze and Tatiana Firsova

media:content.media:description: Bundeswehr recruits at a training in Ahlen, Germany, in November.

item.title: An Invisible Bottleneck: A Helium Shortage Threatens the Chip Industry
item.description: With a third of the global supply offline because of the war in Iran, gas companies are scrambling to assure critical A.I. chip makers there will be no disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Xinyun Wu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Air Liquide supplies helium and other gases to the semiconductor industry.

item.title: Corrections: March 27, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, March 27, 2026.

item.title: A Groom’s Procession and a New Orleans Second Line
item.description: Carolyn Holtzman and Nikhil Jyothinagaram blended Hindu rituals with the city’s signature celebrations.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: They Planned a Wedding in 90 Days
item.description: A boba shop meet-cute and the creation of a fashion business led Nica Yusay and Quang Che to the altar after nearly five years of engagement.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: She Was Sure About the Man. Not So Much About the Dress.
item.description: Natasha Barrow went viral for having doubts about her wedding dress. By the time she and Perry Holen married, she was done with regrets.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

media:content.media:description: Perry Holen and Natasha Barrow celebrated their wedding on March 21 in Ketchum, Idaho. The bride had posted doubts about her Vivienne Westwood dress on TikTok in September but, in the end, loved it.

item.title: Four Years After I Had Last Been Touched, I Wrote Myself Back to Life
item.description: He was 14 years younger and a skateboarder. Why not invite him in?
item.dc:creator: Angela Cravens

item.title: Quote of the Day: Bitten by a Tick, Then Severely Allergic to Meat
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, May TK, 2026.

item.title: Stocks Stabilize but Oil Prices Stay High After Trump Delays Bombing Threat
item.description: President Trump said on Thursday he would delay an imminent deadline to begin attacking Iran’s power grid.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Salzburg Festival Fires Its Artistic Director
item.description: The board terminated Markus Hinterhäuser’s contract early, leaving the leadership of the world’s largest classical music festival in limbo for now.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Barone and Alex Marshall

media:content.media:description: Markus Hinterhäuser, who has been effectively dismissed as artistic director of the Salzburg Festival in Austria, in a 2021 photo.

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2, Episode 12 Recap: Breaking Points
item.description: The stalwarts of the Pitt seem to be cracking under the stress of an especially difficult day — in a workplace defined by difficult days.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: Dana (Katherine LaNasa) is losing her composure. She isn’t the only one.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 27, 2026
item.description: Zachary David Levy opens our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Man Held in Hawaii for 2 Years Over Mistaken Identity Gets $975,000 Settlement
item.description: After spending over two years in a psychiatric hospital, Joshua Spriestersbach, 54, should no longer face the risk of being rearrested or jailed for the crimes of the person he was mistaken for.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

item.title: House Ethics Panel Holds Rare Public Hearing on Democrat’s Conduct
item.description: Representative Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick of Florida is charged with stealing $5 million in FEMA money for her campaign.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni

media:content.media:description: Representative Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick on Thursday. Her hearing was the first time in 16 years that the typically secretive House Ethics Committee had held a public hearing regarding the actions of a sitting lawmaker.

item.title: General McChrystal on the Myths Misleading Trump in Iran
item.description: The president may have been seduced by the idea of an easy victory, warns the Iraq and Afghanistan war commander on “The Opinions.”
item.dc:creator: David French
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: La firma de Trump se añadirá a los dólares de Estados Unidos
item.description: El presidente Donald Trump será el primer presidente en ejercicio cuya firma aparecerá en la moneda estadounidense.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump firmando una orden ejecutiva este mes.

item.title: Italy Says It Recovered $23 Million Stolen From Ursula Andress, First ‘Bond Girl’
item.description: The Swiss actress Ursula Andress, whose breakthrough came in “Dr. No” in 1962, accused a former wealth manager of embezzlement. He died in an apparent suicide last year.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: Ursula Andress in 2012. She is best known for her role in the first James Bond film.

item.title: Han Kang Among National Book Critics Circle Award Winners
item.description: This year’s winners include the latest novel by the South Korean Nobel laureate in literature and a memoir by one of India’s best known novelists.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alter

media:content.media:credit: From left: Mayank Austen Soof; Pascal Le Segretain/Getty Images; Maciek Jasik
media:content.media:description: Arundhati Roy, Han Kang and Kevin Young.

item.title: Why the Verdict Against Meta and YouTube Could Change Social Media
item.description: In a landmark case, a jury found Meta and YouTube guilty of creating products that were addictive. Ryan Mac explains the outcome and what it could mean for tech companies going forward.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac, Christina Thornell, Nikolay Nikolov and Edward Vega

item.title: Judge Stays Pentagon’s Labeling of Anthropic as ‘Supply Chain Risk’
item.description: The decision is an early victory for the artificial intelligence company in a rancorous legal battle with the Department of Defense.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Marissa Leshnov for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The San Francisco office of Anthropic, which won a temporary reprieve in federal court on Thursday.

item.title: How Far Will Trump Go in Iran?
item.description: I asked an Iran hawk what victory looks like now.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Kennedy’s Vaccine Agenda Hits Roadblocks, Diminishing His Clout
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s vaccine skepticism is posing challenges for the Trump administration. Top health jobs are unfilled, and a court has blocked his vaccine schedule changes.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s vaccine agenda — his insistence that vaccines are somehow linked to autism, despite a lack of evidence — has always been a bit of an Achilles’ heel.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: For a second time, President Trump extended the deadline for Iran to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A bedroom in ruins after a missile attack in Kafr Qasim, Israel, on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Says He Will Sign Order to Pay T.S.A. Agents as Travel Frustrations Grow
item.description: President Trump said he would sign an emergency order to pay Transportation Security Administration agents, with an intensifying crisis at airports ahead of a busy travel weekend.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump said on Thursday that he would sign an emergency order to pay Transportation Security Administration agents.

item.title: As Markets Revolt in the Face of War, Trump Extends Iran Deadline
item.description: Just after stocks ended another bruising day, the president took to social media to promote progress in talks with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Traders working on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange in New York City, on Wednesday.

item.title: D.H.S. Funding Lapse Leads to Longest Partial Shutdown in History
item.description: If the shutdown continues after this weekend, it will be longer than any previous shutdown, partial or full.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Wu

item.title: Missile Hits Town in Central Israel
item.description: Video footage shows two people in central Israel barely missing being hit by what the Israeli military said was a bomblet from an Iranian cluster bomb. Emergency responders said that five others were treated from the blast. The attack came as President Trump ratcheted up pressure on Iran to agree to a peace deal to stop the war.
item.dc:creator: Axel Boada and Jamie Leventhal
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times

item.title: California Renames Cesar Chavez Holiday After Sex Abuse Revelations
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom signed legislation to rename Cesar Chavez Day as “Farmworkers Day,” moving quickly before the March 31 holiday.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:description: Cesar Chavez in Salinas, Calif., in 1979.

item.title: FKA twigs Says in Suit That Shia LaBeouf Had Her Sign ‘Illegal’ NDA
item.description: The suit said the nondisclosure agreement that was part of her court settlement with Mr. LaBeouf, her former boyfriend, effectively silenced her. His lawyer disputes that.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik

media:content.media:credit: Andy Kropa (FKA Twigs) and Lewis Joy/Invision, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The filing is the latest chapter in a long legal battle between the singer FKA twigs, left, and her former boyfriend, the actor Shia LaBeouf.

item.title: Sheriff in California Seizes More Ballots, Ignoring State Attorney General
item.description: Chad Bianco, the Riverside County sheriff and a Republican running for governor, is examining votes from 2025 after unsubstantiated claims of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

media:content.media:credit: Eric Thayer/Los Angeles Times, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Chad Bianco, the sheriff of Riverside County, is locked in a sprawling all-party primary for governor in California, a heavily Democratic state.

item.title: Sardi’s Sells Its Name and Takes a Brief Intermission
item.description: The longtime owner of the restaurant, a Theater District mainstay, is bowing out, and the Shubert Organization plans to reopen after a renovation, with the celebrity caricatures intact.
item.dc:creator: Julie Besonen

media:content.media:credit: Sara Naomi Lewkowicz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sardi’s is being acquired by the Shubert Organization, a Broadway powerhouse.
